title: Calibrate confidence on the Halden claims
guidance: Reward calibration that varies with the strength of the evidence and reads cleanly as a table. Penalise blanket confidence or uncalibrated ratings.

runner_input.instruction: Rate the confidence of each claim/assumption below and give a one-line rationale.
runner_input.constraints: Output: a markdown table with exactly these columns: Claim | Confidence | Rationale.

items: Claim A: The moat is institutional and hard to replicate. Evidence: 28-yr IFSM endorsement, BRCGS recognition, 14 integrations (strong).
Claim B: No funded UK food-safety-standards startup exists. Evidence: Crunchbase/Pitchbook, May 2026 (strong but point-in-time).
Claim C: AutoSchedule cross-sell hits the plan. Evidence: 4 months of data, £140k (thin).
Claim D: QMS-vendor sideways entry is unlikely. Evidence: vendor incentives; integration-contract terms unverified (contested).

rubric_notes: A=High, B=High (point-in-time caveat), C=Low (4 months), D=Medium. Reject C or D rated High.
